competitors.name: Influence.co
features: Provides rate ranges based on follower count and engagement
Free to use with no signup required
weaknesses: Very basic inputs (only followers/engagement)
No customization for niche, content type, or production value
No ability to save or export calculations
No guidance on negotiation or contract terms

competitors.name: Social Bluebook
features: Offers a marketplace for connecting brands and creators
weaknesses: Requires signup/email to see detailed rates
Feels brand-centric (tool is for brands to 'value' creators)
Rates can feel low or demoralizing to creators
Complex interface with upsells to its core marketplace service

competitors.name: Grin (Creator Rates)
features: Backed by industry data and surveys
Breaks down rates by platform and content type
weaknesses: Not a true interactive calculator; it's a static report/PDF
Data is aggregated and not personalized
No ability to input your own metrics
Serves primarily as a lead magnet for their enterprise software

competitors.name: Fohr Card
features: Asks for specific metrics (reach, saves, likes)
weaknesses: Extremely simplistic output (one number)
No breakdown of what the rate includes
No context on how the rate was derived
No advice on how to use the rate in negotiations

competitors.name: NoxInfluencer Calculator
features: Can pull data directly from a channel URL
Part of a larger suite of influencer analytics tools
weaknesses: Only for YouTube, ignoring other platforms
Interface is cluttered and not intuitive
Primary goal is to upsell to paid analytics
The 'sponsorship rate' is buried among other monetization metrics

gaps.gap: Lack of a holistic, customizable calculator that accounts for value beyond vanity metrics.
gaps.implementation: Build a calculator with advanced inputs: niche premium (e.g., finance vs. lifestyle), content production cost (hours, equipment), exclusivity period, usage rights, and past campaign performance data. Use these to generate a detailed, itemized rate card.

gaps.gap: No tool provides actionable negotiation support or contract guidance alongside the rate.
gaps.implementation: After generating a rate, offer dynamic negotiation scripts, a checklist of standard contract clauses to review/request, and templates for pitches and invoices. This moves from 'what to charge' to 'how to get it.'

gaps.gap: Missing integration with creator platforms for automatic data import and rate tracking over time.
gaps.implementation: Allow creators to connect their YouTube Studio, Instagram Professional Dashboard, or TikTok Analytics to auto-populate metrics. Let them save historical calculations to track rate growth and campaign history.

gaps.gap: All tools are designed for one-off calculations, not for managing an entire sponsorship business.
gaps.implementation: Create a 'Sponsorship Rate Manager' dashboard where creators can set different rates for different platforms/content types, create and send custom proposals, and track conversations with brands—all centered around their calculated baseline rates.

gaps.gap: No tool effectively serves nano/micro-influencers with high engagement but low follower counts, who are often most confused about pricing.
gaps.implementation: Create a calculator and educational content specifically framed for this audience, emphasizing value-per-engagement and case studies. Offer a 'confidence slider' that adjusts the rate based on the creator's own perceived value, backed by coaching tips.

recommended_positioning: The first AI-powered rate calculator & negotiation platform that helps creators build custom, defensible rate cards and close better sponsorship deals.
